mnemonics: UNSUPERVISED NEIGHBORS ORGANIZE THEMSELVES: Unsupervised learning algorithms where neighboring nodes organize themselves through competition and cooperation to form meaningful patterns without external labels.
keyPoints: Self-Organizing Maps (SOMs) use competitive learning to produce a low-dimensional representation of input space.
The algorithm adjusts weights of winning neurons and their neighbors to preserve topological properties.
Common applications include data visualization, clustering, and dimensionality reduction.
commonMistakes: Confusing SOMs with supervised learning; they are unsupervised and don't require labeled data.
Forgetting that neighborhood function shrinks over time, which is crucial for convergence.
Misunderstanding the grid structure; it represents topological relationships, not necessarily spatial ones.
